title: Your very first impressions of Evangelion
display_title: Your very first impressions of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Your very first impressions of Evangelion             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Feb 26, 2009 10:02 pm So what everyone's very first ideas of Evangelion were, before you knew anything about the story. It might have been seeing a picture or front cover somewhere, or music video on Youtube? Basically the first time you saw anything NGE related, what did you think it was all about? For me I was six when I first saw the series and I did not care about it until 2005 when It was Adult Swim and it came on after the Uncut Season 2 of DBZ. I thought it must just be another traditional Robot series like Transformers (G1 or Armada series) or G Gundam and the only thing I knew about the series that I saw it was kid and their was a Gaint Pruple Robot fighting a Monster with some blades on it hands and some Blue Hair Chick was part of the series also. It wasn't until 2007 when I got into it more and was wrong about everything. P.S Sorry if this topic was made before. If it was then lock this one and give me the link to the older topic.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Feb 26, 2009 10:28 pm My first impression was that it was about a secret facility that cloned people.

[]:             Quote Post by Dramamine » Thu Feb 26, 2009 10:54 pm Well, about six years ago, being the lil' lad that I was, I purchased "Sonic the Hedgehog: The Movie" from a local dvd store. After I finished nostalgiaing at the spiny bastard's speed and whatnot, I decided to scan through the previews of various anime that accompanied the disk. That was my first glimpse at Neon Genesis Evangelion--the preview was the "ADV Ode to Joy trailer" Located here: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IOhu6a9hUq4 I watched that same trailer over fifty times, after each viewing I kept repeating, "I must see this." Alas, I had no way of achieving that goal. The years passed, and I came upon Sonic the Hedgehog again... and the trailer, however this time I followed through with my previous wishes. I just watched that same trailer right now, and can recall the exact feeling of astonishment I had as a child.

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Thu Feb 26, 2009 11:04 pm My first impression was reading about in a magazine, and being completely clueless about it. So i got to watch the series as if I never heard of Evangelion. That was a ton of fun

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 27, 2009 2:20 am Mid-late '90s : "Appleseed was great, and Shirow's other stuff not bad ... I wonder if any of this other Japanese stuff is as good." That was when I put the first chapter of the manga back on the shelf. Early '00s : "This Evangelion thing ... I sorta remember it as having a rep for being one of the better giant robot shows. And I'm running out of girls-with-guns titles..." That was when I picked up the DVD. I managed to be blissfully unspoiled.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Fri Feb 27, 2009 3:16 am I wrote: Summer 1999. My friend had the series on VHS and we marathoned it. A month or so later we got our hands on a VHS fansub of EoE. It was the only anime other than DBZ and Akira I'd seen. I think it resonated with me because I was 14 at the time and I thought "Oh wow, I can totally relate. This is so fucking DEEP" &c.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Fri Feb 27, 2009 3:45 am I first saw the ADV "Ode to Joy" trailer on a Slayers dvd. The exact thing I thought at that time was "If I saw this, I would never understand it completely" because of all the angels, evas, and different characters. The first Evangelion video that I saw was Death and Rebirth, where originally I said (the usual) "These staircases go on forever" or "There is an unusual amount of kanji," or "These are strange camera angles." But these impressions faded completely after watching the episodes (thankfully).

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Feb 27, 2009 9:23 am I found it on youtube and watched it. I didn't know what to expect.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Feb 27, 2009 10:27 am I knew there was an anime called Evangelion for a few years but never gave it any thought so I didn't have an impression of it. Then one day I caught an english dubbed episode on TV, i think Adult Swim- Episode 8, the intro of Asuka- and I thought: It's cool looking, but kinda juvenile. Nothing worth looking it. They were playing one episode a week at the moment so I missed out on it for a while because the next episode I saw was Episode 16- Lelliel, and that just blew me away! It really hit an emotional cord. I researched the series intensely, caught Episodes 17 & 18 on TV, loved them even more and thencouldn't wait and (in a leap of faith) spent over $150 (at the time) to buy the Platinum thinpack and the Movie Boxset. So I guess you could say my first impression of Eva was "Big Deal"

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Fri Feb 27, 2009 3:39 pm I saw a trailer in a Robotech DVD I got a long while back. I thought to myself, "Damn, there's a lot of stupid cross-shaped explosions." Then I heard about it again years later and actually started watching it. I however, did very little research and saw the movie before I finished the series.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Feb 27, 2009 4:13 pm I liked it. Bearing in mind it was only the third anime I'd ever seen (after Ghost in the Shell Movie, and Elfen Lied). A lot of the more complex aspects of the series passed me by, so I just enjoyed the ride until I got to EOE and EOTV which was when I realised I'd found something very special. So I re-watched it a few more time to better understand it.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Feb 28, 2009 4:36 pm I got to know Evangelion via a T-shirt my grandmother gave me, she obviously not having a clue as to what it was. I then grew curious, spoiled it for myself on the Internet and decided to follow it through by watching it. Not the general order you'd do things in, but I'm like that. I loved it. Still do. Still my favourite, off my general anime/media rating scale.

[]:             Quote Post by raust » Sun Mar 01, 2009 12:53 pm I first heard of Evangelion from a friend. He gave me a brief synopsis of the series. I thought that it was going to be another apocalyptic knock off series that has been done before. I watched the series a week later and it was like nothing that I've seen before.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Mar 01, 2009 7:35 pm I saw episode 19 on Adult Swim, first. I don't think I need to say anything else.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Mar 01, 2009 10:02 pm Apart from the ADV trailer and Death and Rebirth, the first volume of the manga once caught my eye. However, I did not initially follow through with buying it because I thought the headpieces were supposed to be cat ears...(I know, I know)

[]:             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Mon Mar 02, 2009 1:26 pm CorporalChaos wrote: most of these threads lack neko...

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Mon Mar 02, 2009 2:14 pm When I first read about it, the article only cited that Evangelion was considered the best anime ever. Remember Gundam? That's what I thought Eva would be (and I hadn't even seen Gundam back then, I only took a look at it recently).

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Mar 02, 2009 8:26 pm I saw it on the cover of Animerica back in '98 and bought the first VHS (two episodes). Mind was blown, and it felt like the VHSes took forever to come out. My anticipation for EoE was unbearable.

[]:             Quote Post by nozunam » Tue Mar 03, 2009 12:50 pm at thought the show was boring, everyone was just talking. but there was nothing else to do at that time the show was airing. then comes the episode where Shinji first visits Rei in her apartment. this very scene opened my eyes to hentai... and that's how I got watching hentai back then... wait... this isn't about how u got watching hentai is it?

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Mar 03, 2009 3:04 pm Il n'y a pas d'hentai dans l'evangelion...! Everywhere else though.
